seoTitle: How to Create a Form in Typeform Step by Step
metaDescription: Learn how to build a Typeform from scratch, choose a template, add questions, configure logic, and publish it to collect responses.
intro: Building a professional online form doesn't need to be complicated when you know the basic workflow. This guide walks through creating a form in Typeform from scratch, choosing a template, adding questions, configuring logic, and publishing it to start collecting responses.

steps.heading: Create a new form
steps.body: Start a new Typeform by choosing a template that fits your purpose, such as a lead form or survey, or begin from a blank form.

steps.heading: Add questions and customize design
steps.body: Add the question types you need and adjust the theme, colors, and branding so the form matches your style.

steps.heading: Configure logic and settings
steps.body: Set up conditional logic if you want the form to adapt based on answers, and review the general form settings before publishing.

steps.heading: Publish and share your form
steps.body: Publish the finished form and share it through a link, embed, or integration to start collecting responses.

faq.question: Should I start with a template or a blank form?
faq.answer: Templates can save time by giving you a starting structure for common use cases, while a blank form offers more flexibility if your needs are specific.

faq.question: What kinds of forms can I build with Typeform?
faq.answer: You can build lead generation forms, customer feedback surveys, registrations, applications, and contact forms, among others.

faq.question: Can I customize the form's design before publishing?
faq.answer: Yes, Typeform lets you adjust themes, colors, and branding as part of building the form before it goes live.

faq.question: Do I need to configure logic for every form?
faq.answer: No, logic is optional and mainly useful when you want the form to change based on how a respondent answers earlier questions.
